blocks.content: Davenport addressed the full group again before leaving for the morning.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: We're going to be looking at two lines of inquiry simultaneously. Mr. Price and his potential involvement. And the circumstances within this house over the weekend. I want to be clear — we haven't concluded anything about anyone in this room. What I need from all of you is availability and honesty. Nothing else.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: When will you have the full medical examiner's report.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Some preliminary findings this week. Full toxicology takes time. I'll share what I can, when I can.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: We'd like you to remain local through at least end of week. If that changes I'll let you know.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: End of week. That's five more days.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Yes. I understand that's an imposition. I appreciate your cooperation.
He and Serrano left. The door closed. The five of them sat in the living room they had been sitting in for going on two days now and the walls felt closer than they had the day before.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: Don't be. It's not your fault.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: That's a very generous reading of the situation.
